data_method: H-1B LCA new approvals FY2024 (~12k WA initial-employment per USCIS) + LinkedIn new-hire signals (workforce reports) + levels.fyi 2023-25 self-reported starts. Renter share from ACS PUMS age-22-29 + bachelor's-or-higher + computer-occupation tenure proxy.
notes: Highest concentration in Class A urban Bellevue/SLU/Downtown. Layoff elasticity 1.8 means a 10% layoff event drives ~18% vacancy in their primary submarkets (Bellevue Class A vacancy rose ~430bps Q1-Q3 2023 per CoStar). Screening friction: thin US credit, recent visa transfers. Will accept higher rent for shorter leases. Owner conversion low (3%/yr) because savings runway is short.

data_method: ACS PUMS age 26-35 + 5-figure stock vest cohort approximation + levels.fyi tenure-band self-report + Microsoft+Amazon+Google retention curve estimates (~70% 4-yr retention).
notes: Cohort B is the home-purchase pipeline. 12% annual owner conversion is the rental-demand drain. Sammamish/Sammamish Plateau (98074, 98075) and Kirkland (98033) are the buy-up destinations. Mid-tenure renters still in Bellevue/Redmond core often have RSU vest peaks driving down-payment. Lower layoff elasticity because more savings + higher tenure-protective severance.

data_method: ACS PUMS age 30-50 + computer-occupation + 8+ yr tenure + Microsoft equity-vest projections. Owner-conversion is endpoint; this cohort is mostly owners. Renter share ~28% concentrates in dual-tech-couple holdouts in walkable Eastside or Capitol Hill.
notes: Accidental-landlord conversion (Cohort C owners who relocate but retain WA property) is part of the supply side. Senior owner conversion rate is low because most have already bought. Layoff elasticity 0.4: senior layoffs deliver large severance + asset cushion + selective re-employment.

data_method: USCIS H-1B Employer Data Hub continuing approvals FY2024 + I-797 active status estimate. Two-county allocation: Microsoft+Amazon+Meta+Google+Nvidia+Anthropic+Databricks+Snowflake H-1B counts. Renter share ~96% (visa-tied rarely buy due to portability concern + 60-day grace period exposure).
notes: Highest screening friction (68/100): thin US credit, visa-portability risk, no co-signer, employment-status documentation gap. Highest layoff elasticity (2.1) because layoff = 60-day grace period = forced exit from US. Cohort D bridges Cohort A+B; many Cohort A members are also Cohort D. Sub-market clustering 98052+98004+98008+98007 reflects Microsoft+Amazon+Meta Bellevue/Redmond + body-shop staffing (TCS, Infosys) at same sites. Some overlap with Cohort A (~40%).

data_method: layoffs.fyi + WARN affected counts 2024-2025 (~14k 2024 + ~6k 2025 YTD) net of re-employment (assumed 45% within 90 days based on Indeed Hiring Lab + LinkedIn data). Severance + unemployment + COBRA window 4-6 months typical.
notes: Severance + 0%-interest savings extends rent capacity 3-6 months; after that, downshift Class A → Class B/C or region-exit. Region-exit share ~22% (move to lower-cost metro). Owner conversion 1% (few buy in this window; some forced sells of recent purchases). Screening friction highest (82) for new applications: employment gap, severance-as-income complexity. Layoff elasticity 5.2 means cohort E swells 5x for every 10% layoff event.

data_method: BLS QCEW NAICS 5415 (computer services) + 5417 (R&D services) self-employed share + 1099-MISC W-2 contractor allocations (estimated ~28% of NAICS workforce in 2-county region) + Upwork/Freelancer top contractor concentrations. ACS PUMS self-employed + computer occupation cross-tab.
notes: Includes freelance designers, contract SDEs, body-shop placements (TCS, Infosys, Wipro contractors are mostly H-1B Cohort D but some are 1099 - allocate ~30% of Cohort D's TCS-managed workforce to Cohort F overlap). Income variable, harder underwriting (screening friction 55). Layoff elasticity 1.4: contractor demand falls sharply in downturn but rebounds faster than FTE.
